commands.review_summary:
The PR changes watch-capture transient ScreenCaptureKit retry backoff to use the existing stop-aware sleep helper, adds a focused Swift regression test, and includes a release-note line.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main and the latest release show raw `Task.sleep` in the transient retry branch, and the PR body/comment provide terminal timing proof plus a focused injected regression.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because no actionable patch defect was found; the remaining action is CI completion and maintainer review.

target.title: fix(capture): honor stop during watch transient backoff
target.body:
## Summary

Watch capture sessions could ignore `requestStop()` for up to 350ms while backing off from a transient ScreenCaptureKit failure. The transient retry path used raw `Task.sleep`, unlike the other sleeps in the loop.

This routes transient backoff through the existing stop-aware sleep helper with a retry-local timestamp, preserving the full retry delay while allowing a stop request to wake it immediately.

## Regression coverage

The focused test now:

1. captures one valid frame so normal session finalization remains meaningful;
2. waits for the next capture to enter the transient-failure backoff;
3. requests stop inside that 350ms window; and
4. requires the session to finish within 80ms.

Maintainer review removed the standalone 198-line source-mutating proof harness; the production-matched Swift regression is the durable proof.

## Verification

```text
swift test --package-path Core/PeekabooCore --no-parallel --filter 'Stop request wakes transient capture backoff'
PROOF transient_stop_elapsed_ms=9
1 test passed

swift test --package-path Core/PeekabooCore --no-parallel --filter WatchCaptureSessionTests
PROOF transient_stop_elapsed_ms=12
15 tests passed
```

- SwiftFormat: clean on the changed Swift files
- `git diff --check`: clean
- Codex autoreview of the maintainer fixup: clean
- Codex autoreview of the full final branch against `origin/main`: clean

## Caveat

A live ScreenCaptureKit transient denial is not deterministic enough for a stable test. The regression injects the same transient error only after a valid frame and synchronizes on entry to the retry path.

## Related

- Prior transient-capture retry work: #135

commands.command: > proof: 🧂 unranked krab — blocked until real behavior proof is added. added real terminal behavior proof and tightened the regression. **proof command (any macos host with swift):** ```bash bash scripts/prove-watch-transient-stop.sh ``` **observed output on this machine:** ```text watch_transient_backoff_delay_ms=350 stop_requested_after_backoff_start_ms=5 raw_task_sleep_stop_elapsed_ms=369 stop_aware_sleep_stop_elapsed_ms=6 ``` same stop timing (5ms into the 350ms backoff window): unfixed raw `task.sleep` still runs ~369ms; the stop-aware helper exits in ~6ms. that is the behavioral difference this pr wires into `watchcapturesession+loop.swift`. also tightened `stop request wakes transient capture backoff` to assert stop within **80ms** (was 1s) and to synchronize stop during the backoff window. the proof script step 2 runs red/green against that test when full xcode is available. pr body now includes a `## real behavior proof` section with the captured output. @clawsweeper re-review
commands.freeform_prompt: > Proof: 🧂 unranked krab — blocked until real behavior proof is added. Added real terminal behavior proof and tightened the regression. **Proof command (any macOS host with Swift):** ```bash bash scripts/prove-watch-transient-stop.sh ``` **Observed output on this machine:** ```text watch_transient_backoff_delay_ms=350 stop_requested_after_backoff_start_ms=5 raw_task_sleep_stop_elapsed_ms=369 stop_aware_sleep_stop_elapsed_ms=6 ``` Same stop timing (5ms into the 350ms backoff window): unfixed raw `Task.sleep` still runs ~369ms; the stop-aware helper exits in ~6ms. That is the behavioral difference this PR wires into `WatchCaptureSession+Loop.swift`. Also tightened `Stop request wakes transient capture backoff` to assert stop within **80ms** (was 1s) and to synchronize stop during the backoff window. The proof script step 2 runs red/green against that test when full Xcode is available. PR body now includes a `## Real behavior proof` section with the captured output. @clawsweeper re-review
